contract.name: v2/disaster/agency/count.md
contract.description: Documentation reports this POST endpoint should return a count of toptier agencies associated with disaster or emergency funding filtered by supplied DEF codes; runtime behavior could not be verified due to connection resets.

filter.description: Wrapper object expected by the published documentation; docs state it should contain disaster funding filters such as `def_codes`.
filter.constraints: Structure inferred solely from documentation; live validation unavailable.

filter.def_codes.description: Documentation claims this array enumerates Disaster Emergency Fund codes to include (examples show values like 'L', 'M', 'N').
filter.def_codes.constraints: Marked as required in docs but requirement is unverified.

filter.award_type_codes.description: Documentation notes this optional array limits the count to awards matching the provided award type codes.
filter.award_type_codes.constraints: Acceptable values list comes from documentation and remains unconfirmed.

count.description: Documentation example shows a numeric count of agencies; no live responses were observed.

quirks: API host repeatedly terminates TLS handshakes without returning HTTP status or body, preventing contract confirmation.
Hitting analogous paths on https://www.usaspending.gov succeeds but serves cached HTML rather than the JSON API.
risks: Integrations may require allowlisting, mTLS, or other undisclosed network prerequisites to access api.usaspending.gov.
Relying on documentation alone risks schema drift because no live responses have been captured.
gaps: No successful response captured; true response shape, status codes, and error formats are unknown.
Cannot confirm whether documentation-described request validation (e.g., required def_codes) is enforced.

probes.notes: Connectivity check returned 'Empty reply from server'; no HTTP response received.
Minimal payload per documentation; server closed connection with 'Empty reply from server'.
GET attempt ended with connection close and no response body.
Request to https://www.usaspending.gov/api/v2/status/ returned cached HTML shell rather than JSON.

mismatches: Documentation describes a JSON response with an agency count, yet every probe to https://api.usaspending.gov closed the connection without any HTTP response.
Even when using alternative clients and headers, the API host never emitted the documented JSON payloads.
gaps: Unclear whether additional authentication, network prerequisites, or headers are required to receive responses.
Actual request validation rules, error formats, and successful response schema remain undocumented by probes.
risks: Production integrations could experience total outages if the host requires allowlisting or mTLS not documented.
Implementations based solely on documentation may diverge from reality due to lack of runtime verification.
